audit_metadata.title: TECHNICAL AUDIT REPORT
audit_metadata.executive_summary: This technical audit identifies raw technical findings only. No prioritization, remediation plan, effort estimation, dependencies, or roadmap are included. All findings are initialized with 'Open' status.

evidence.snippet:
DATABASE_PASSWORD = 'prod_db_pass_2026'
JWT_SECRET = 'super_secret_key_6789!'

findings.description: Application database credentials and signature keys are stored directly inside the source code repository.

evidence.snippet:
query = f'SELECT * FROM users WHERE custom_filter = {user_input}'
cursor.execute(query)

findings.description: Database queries are dynamically constructed through raw user input concatenation without parameterization.

evidence.snippet: allow_origins=['*']

findings.description: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing is configured with a wildcard, allowing unverified external domains to trigger actions.

evidence.snippet: No rate-limiting decorators or middleware attached to /api/v1/auth/login

findings.description: The login routing endpoint exposes unlimited authentication check iterations, vulnerable to brute-force attacks.

evidence.snippet: return hashlib.md5(password.encode()).hexdigest()

findings.description: User storage access relies on the obsolete MD5 hashing algorithm instead of secure key derivation functions like bcrypt.

evidence.snippet:
@router.post('/purge')
async def purge_logs(current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)):

findings.description: Sensitive administrative endpoints verify user identity but omit specific role mapping checks (e.g., is_admin).

evidence.snippet: FastAPI instance lacks SecureCookies, X-XSS-Protection, or Content-Security-Policy configurations.

findings.description: The server response does not populate essential HTTP security headers needed to prevent XSS and clickjacking.

evidence.snippet: High sequential scan rate on filtering columns: tenant_id, category_id, created_at

findings.description: High-frequency filtering keys are queried continuously without indices, creating resource-intensive sequential scans.

evidence.snippet: SELECT * FROM items; -> followed by 100 individual queries: SELECT * FROM categories WHERE id = x;

findings.description: Iterating over nested entities triggers secondary lookup queries instead of performing an efficient eager SQL join operation.

evidence.snippet:
connection = psycopg2.connect(...)
# Opened and closed explicitly on every HTTP request

findings.description: The persistence framework constructs an isolated database link per request cycle without utilizing connection pool structures.

evidence.snippet: Execution time: 3.4 seconds for intensive count/sum matrix grouping runs.

findings.description: Heavy complex relational operations are recalculated entirely on-demand on raw data without server-side caching.

evidence.snippet: return db.query(Order).all() # No limit or offset bindings

findings.description: Data pipelines return arbitrary table volumes to client spaces without applying page, range, or size limits.

evidence.snippet:
create_order_record(data)
decrement_inventory_stock(data) # Occurs outside an atomic block context

findings.description: Multistep related write sequences run independently without transactional wrapper execution, risking database desynchronization.

evidence.snippet: File size: 2,540 lines of code handling 22 discrete endpoints across 4 distinct domains.

findings.description: A dense monolith module merges authentication routing, storage workflows, and pricing algorithms, reducing maintainability.
Broad catch-all constructs suppress system-wide unexpected runtime errors silently without tracking execution stacks.

evidence.snippet:
async def update_status(order_id, payload):
    return raw_dict_response

findings.description: Core routing routines skip Python type hints and bypass structural Pydantic validation input/output guarantees.

evidence.snippet: Identical phone/email regex matches written separately inside users.py, vendors.py, and clients.py

findings.description: Input schema parsing criteria contain explicit duplicate blocks across domains rather than centralizing structural schemas.

evidence.snippet: Module contains 5 parsing functions with zero references across active repository modules.

findings.description: Unused legacy processing tools remain inside the code repository, raising clutter metrics.

evidence.snippet: Mix of camelCase fields (userId) and snake_case properties (created_at) inside database structural maps.

findings.description: Property names alternate inconsistently between case styles across persistence objects, breaking structural style guides.

evidence.snippet: final_price = base_price * 1.206 # What does 1.206 stand for?

findings.description: Business scaling algorithms use raw numeric literals inline instead of naming variables as isolated explicit constants.

evidence.snippet: Stripe payment and warehouse processing tracks show 0.0% coverage execution logs.

findings.description: Overall code path validation sits below 12%, missing integration checks for business-critical payment funnels.

evidence.snippet: response = httpx.post('https://api.dhl.com/v1/shipments', json=payload)

findings.description: Local test modules request live endpoint executions on third-party domains, causing environment fragility.

evidence.snippet: Total absence of Locust, K6, or JMeter benchmarking configuration manifests.

findings.description: The workspace does not run high-volume concurrent request tools to find performance drop points before deployment.

evidence.snippet: No github/workflows directory, .gitlab-ci.yml, or Jenkinsfile present.

findings.description: The platform relies on manual server deployments, missing automated test runs on branch push cycles.

evidence.snippet: PyJWT==1.7.1 (Vulnerable to CVE-2022-39174 - Remote Code Execution threat)

findings.description: Third-party libraries use legacy versions that contain known critical security vulnerabilities.

evidence.snippet: No Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml available in the root repository path.

findings.description: Runtime environments use manual bare metal hosting setups, creating environment mismatch bugs.

evidence.snippet: UPLOAD_DIR = 'C:\Users\Administrator\Uploads\'

findings.description: Local storage targets are tied to absolute host directories, which breaks execution when moved to Linux servers.

evidence.snippet: No .env.example, config.yaml, or distinct dev/staging/prod split setups.

findings.description: The application lacks environment-specific runtime states, risking dev tasks connecting to production databases.

evidence.snippet: print(f'Error occurred inside database operation: {err}')

findings.description: System monitoring tracks events using basic print statements, lacking log levels or indexable metadata.

evidence.snippet: Swagger UI populates routing paths but lists all responses under generic '200 OK' schemas with blank fields.

findings.description: Auto-generated OpenAPI files omit error response tracking, request schema models, and text descriptions.
